NSAA-2022-M1-001: Which one of the following is a simplification of $y\left(\dfrac{3x^{1/2}z}{y^3}\right)^2$?
NSAA-2022-M1-002: Triangle PQR has a right angle at Q. The point T lies on QR such that QT = (1/4)QR. PT = 6 cm and PR = 12 cm. What is the length of QT, in cm?
NSAA-2022-M1-003: Find the complete set of values of $x$ that satisfy the inequality: $\dfrac{3}{4}(5-x) - \dfrac{1}{2}(6-x) - x < 0$
NSAA-2022-M1-004: I have two fair dice, X and Y, each with six sides. The faces on X are labelled 1, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. The faces on Y are labelled 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 6. I roll the dice together and calculate my total score by adding the number rolled on X to the number rolled on Y. What is the probability that my total score is greater than 9?
NSAA-2022-M1-005: Rob keeps a record of what he earns each day. On Monday, he earned 50% less than he earned on Sunday. On Tuesday, he earned 20% more than he earned on Monday. On Wednesday, he earned 30% less than he earned on Tuesday. On Wednesday, he earned £84. How much did Rob earn on Sunday?
NSAA-2022-M1-006: The nth term of a sequence T is $(n-3)^2$, where $n$ is a positive integer. The nth term of another sequence V is $3n+p$, where $p$ is a constant and $n$ is a positive integer. The 10th term in T is equal to twice the 5th term in V. What is the 4th term in V?
NSAA-2022-M1-007: Which one of the following is a simplification of $\dfrac{5x^2-17x-12}{25x^2-9} \div \dfrac{x^2+x-12}{x^2-x-6}$?
NSAA-2022-M1-008: S is a list of six numbers: 1, 2, $x$, $x+1$, $x+1$, 15, where $2 \le x \le 14$. The mean of S is one more than the median of S. What is the value of $x$?
NSAA-2022-M1-009: A rectangle PQRS has length $(2x-1)$ cm and width $(x+1)$ cm. A larger rectangle is made by adding 3 cm to both the length and the width of PQRS. The larger rectangle has an area of 360 cm². What is the ratio of PQ to PS?
NSAA-2022-M1-010: $t$ is inversely proportional to the square of $w$. $t$ and $w$ are positive numbers. $t=36$ when $w=2\times10^{-2}$. What is the value of $w$ when $t=100$?
NSAA-2022-M1-011: PQRS is a trapezium [DIAGRAM: trapezium PQRS with angle P=30 degrees, QR=40 m, and tan(angle RSQ)=5/8]. What is the length of PS, in metres?
NSAA-2022-M1-012: A cyclist rides along a track to the top of a hill then immediately turns around and descends along the same track to her starting point. She takes 40 minutes at an average speed of 12 kmh⁻¹ to reach the top. Her average speed for the whole journey is 15 kmh⁻¹. What is the average speed of her descent?
NSAA-2022-M1-013: A solid cylinder has radius $r$ cm and height $h$ cm. A cube has side length $3r$ cm. The total surface area of the cylinder is equal to four times the total surface area of the cube. Which of the following is an expression for $h$ in terms of $r$?
NSAA-2022-M1-014: Consider the equation $2x^2+4x+c=0$, where $c$ is a constant. The positive difference between the roots of this equation is $\sqrt{10}$. What is the value of $c$?
NSAA-2022-M1-015: The variables $x$ and $y$ are related by the equation $x=5-\dfrac{2y^3+1}{1-2y^3}$. Which of the following is a rearrangement to make $y$ the subject?
NSAA-2022-M1-016: PQR is a triangle. S and T are points on the sides PQ and PR. ST is parallel to QR. PS = 3 cm, ST = $x$ cm, QS = $(x+2)$ cm, QR = $(2x+1)$ cm [DIAGRAM: triangle PQR with ST parallel to QR, S on PQ, T on PR]. What is the length, in cm, of QR?
NSAA-2022-M1-017: Three different numbers are chosen at random from 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. What is the probability that the three numbers form the three sides of a right-angled triangle?
NSAA-2022-M1-018: P, Q and R are regular polygons. Q has three times as many sides as P. An interior angle of Q is 10° larger than an interior angle of P. R has twice as many sides as Q. How much larger is an interior angle of R than an interior angle of Q, in degrees?
NSAA-2022-M1-019: The point $(-1,5)$ is translated to the point $(3,2)$ by two successive translations. The first translation is by the vector $\binom{3p}{-4p}$. The second translation is by the vector $\binom{q}{-2q}$. What is the value of $p+q$?
NSAA-2022-M1-020: Consider the graphs of the form $y=x^2+2ax+a$. What is the complete range of values of $a$ for which the minimum point of the graph lies above the x-axis?
NSAA-2022-PHY-001: There is a constant current in a conducting wire. A charge of 20 C passes through the wire in 1.5 minutes. An 18 cm straight section of this wire lies in a uniform magnetic field, perpendicular to the field direction. The magnetic field strength is 0.15 T. What is the magnitude of the magnetic force on this section of wire?
NSAA-2022-PHY-002: A rider on a rollercoaster moves very quickly towards a solid wall. While moving, the rider shouts, and hears an echo of the shout from the wall. The echo is quieter than the original shout. How do the amplitude and frequency of the echo heard by the rider compare to the amplitude and frequency of the original shout?
NSAA-2022-PHY-003: A system has two large copper tanks of water connected by a solid cylindrical copper bar. Water in tank 1 is at temperature T1, tank 2 at a higher temperature T2 (T1 < T2 at all times). Consider these changes made independently: (1) increase T1, (2) increase T2, (3) increase the length of the copper bar, (4) increase the diameter of the copper bar. Which two changes each independently result in an increase in the rate of conduction of thermal energy along the copper bar?
NSAA-2022-PHY-004: Two identical resistors are connected in parallel to a 6.0 V battery. The two resistors dissipate a total power of 0.15 W. One of these resistors is removed from the circuit and connected to a 12 V battery. How much charge passes through this resistor in 6.0 minutes?
NSAA-2022-PHY-005: A small piece of space debris of mass 0.10 g strikes the International Space Station at a relative speed of 15 000 ms⁻¹. The piece of debris comes to rest relative to the space station in 0.010 s. What is the average force exerted on the space station by the piece of debris during this time?
NSAA-2022-PHY-006: A block of mass 6.0 kg is pushed along a rough horizontal surface by a constant force of 8.0 N. The block accelerates uniformly from rest. After 4.0 s its velocity is 2.0 ms⁻¹. How much work is done against resistive forces during this 4.0 s?
NSAA-2022-PHY-007: Ultrasound is used to find a crack inside a cuboid block of metal. A probe on the top surface sends a pulse at t=0 ms, which reflects from both the crack and the bottom surface. [DIAGRAM: graph of reflected pulse strength vs time, showing two peaks between 0 and 0.3 ms]. The speed of ultrasound in the metal is 5000 ms⁻¹. What is the distance between the bottom surface of the metal block and the crack?
NSAA-2022-PHY-008: Power is supplied to an electric motor at 0.800 kW. The motor has an efficiency of 60% and is switched on for half an hour. How much energy is wasted during this time?
NSAA-2022-PHY-009: A circuit includes two ammeters and a resistor R in parallel branches, one branch containing a 3.0 Ω resistor. One ammeter reads 3.3 A (total current) and the other reads 1.8 A (current through R). What is the resistance of resistor R?
NSAA-2022-PHY-010: A graph shows potential difference plotted against current for a filament lamp and a resistor [DIAGRAM: V-I graph, lamp curve non-linear, resistor line linear]. The lamp and resistor are connected in parallel to a 6.0 V supply, and the current in the lamp, I, is recorded. In a second circuit, the lamp and resistor are connected in series to the same supply, and the current in the resistor is 0.18 A. The potential difference across the lamp, V, is recorded. What are the values of I in the first circuit and V in the second circuit?
NSAA-2022-PHY-011: A child is bouncing a ball of mass 0.16 kg vertically up and down on a bat. Each time the ball hits the bat the duration of contact is 0.20 s. The speed of the ball immediately before hitting the bat and immediately after it loses contact is 4.0 ms⁻¹. What is the average contact force between the bat and the ball during each collision? (g = 10 N kg⁻¹)
NSAA-2022-PHY-012: A transverse wave on a string has a speed of 500 ms⁻¹. The horizontal distance between two points P and Q on the wave is 4.0 m [DIAGRAM: wave snapshot showing points P, X, Q along the string, X at maximum displacement 6.0mm]. At t=0 ms, point X on the string is at its maximum displacement of 6.0 mm above equilibrium. What is the displacement of point X at t=7.0 ms?
NSAA-2022-PHY-013: A neutral atom Q of a particular element contains a total of 20 particles (protons, neutrons and electrons). The table shows atoms/ions W, X, Y, Z with: W (21 particles, charge 0), X (21 particles, charge -1), Y (20 particles, charge +1), Z (22 particles, charge 0). Which of these could be a different isotope of the same element as Q?
NSAA-2022-PHY-014: Radioactive isotope X undergoes a single beta decay to form stable isotope Y. A sample consists only of X and Y. [DIAGRAM: graph of mass of Y vs time, plateauing at 50g after a long time]. What is the half-life of X?
NSAA-2022-PHY-015: A piece of metal of mass 50 g is at thermal equilibrium in a hot liquid at temperature T. The metal is removed and immediately placed in 100 g of water at 20°C. The water is stirred and reaches a final temperature of 26°C. Specific heat capacities: hot liquid 2000 J kg⁻¹°C⁻¹, metal 350 J kg⁻¹°C⁻¹, water 4200 J kg⁻¹°C⁻¹. What is the temperature T of the hot liquid?
NSAA-2022-PHY-016: A bar magnet is placed at position X close to one end of a coil, on the axis of the coil [DIAGRAM: magnet-coil setup with velocity-time graph showing the magnet moving from X to Y and back to X]. Which graph represents how the induced voltage in the coil changes as the magnet moves?
NSAA-2022-PHY-017: A small slider of mass 30 g is at rest near the bottom of a frictionless slope, in contact with a light uncompressed spring. The spring is compressed by 5.0 cm; released, it causes the slider to rise to a maximum vertical height of 20 cm. The slider is replaced with one of mass 20 g. The spring is now compressed by 15 cm. To what maximum vertical height does this new slider rise? (spring obeys Hooke's law; air resistance negligible)
NSAA-2022-PHY-018: A tall, smooth cylinder contains air at atmospheric pressure 1.00×10⁵ Pa, density 1.20 kg m⁻³. A heavy piston (mass 50.0 kg, cross-sectional area 0.0200 m²) is placed in the top and falls slowly, compressing the air until it rests in equilibrium. What is the density of the air in the cylinder when the piston rests in equilibrium? (g=10 N kg⁻¹, ideal gas, constant temperature)
NSAA-2022-PHY-019: There are two types of earthquake waves, P-waves and S-waves, produced simultaneously and following the same path. P-waves travel at 5.0 kms⁻¹ and S-waves at 3.0 kms⁻¹. A monitoring station detects P-waves 30 s before S-waves. How far have the waves travelled from the source to reach the station?
NSAA-2022-PHY-020: A solid cuboid has mass 32 kg and density 4.0 gcm⁻³. Faces 1, 2 and 3 have different areas. Resting on one face, the pressure is 1.6 Ncm⁻²; on another face, 0.80 Ncm⁻². What is the pressure on the surface when resting on the third face? (g=10 N kg⁻¹)
NSAA-2022-CHEM-001: The following pairs of 0.1 mol dm⁻³ solutions are mixed separately: (1) AgNO3(aq) with NaI(aq); (2) Cl2(aq) with NaI(aq); (3) HCl(aq) with NaOH(aq); (4) MgCl2(aq) with NaBr(aq). Which pair(s) of solutions, when mixed, would produce a visible chemical change?
NSAA-2022-CHEM-002: Some reactions of metal M and its compounds: M heated in oxygen → metal oxide MO; M heated with sulfuric acid → metal sulfate solution MSO4(aq); MSO4(aq) electrolysed with graphite electrodes → metal M. Which one of the following could be the identity of metal M?
NSAA-2022-CHEM-003: Compound X has: melting point -114°C, boiling point -85°C, poor conductivity as a solid, poor conductivity as a liquid, good conductivity in aqueous solution. Which one of the following could be the identity of compound X?
NSAA-2022-CHEM-004: Which of the following statements about losing electrons is/are correct? (1) During electrolysis of a molten binary compound, the ions attracted to the cathode (negative electrode) lose electrons at that electrode. (2) Descending Group 1 from lithium to caesium, the atoms of the elements lose electrons more easily. (3) When a substance is acting as a reducing agent it loses electrons.
NSAA-2022-CHEM-005: Which of the following chemical reactions is/are redox reactions? (1) Ba²⁺(aq) + SO4²⁻(aq) → BaSO4(s); (2) PCl5(s) + H2O(l) → POCl3(l) + 2HCl(aq); (3) KrF2(s) → Kr(g) + F2(g)
NSAA-2022-CHEM-006: Three samples of calcium of different masses were added separately to excess dilute hydrochloric acid; the volume of hydrogen gas released was monitored [DIAGRAM: graph of volume of H2 vs time for powdered, granular, and solid calcium, plateauing at 120, 240, and 360 cm³ respectively]. What is the mass of powdered calcium used? (Ar: Ca=40; 1 mol gas = 24 dm³ at rtp)
NSAA-2022-CHEM-007: Concentrated aqueous solutions of three compounds are electrolysed with inert electrodes. The constituent elements of which of the following compounds may be collected using this process? (1) copper(II) bromide; (2) hydrogen chloride; (3) potassium chloride
NSAA-2022-CHEM-008: 50 cm³ of 0.100 mol dm⁻³ hydrochloric acid has a pH of 1.0. What is the pH of the mixture formed when 450 cm³ of 0.010 mol dm⁻³ calcium hydroxide solution is added?
NSAA-2022-CHEM-009: Carboxylic acid X reacts with propanol in the presence of an acid catalyst to form compound Y. Compound Y has a relative molar mass of 116. What is the relative molar mass (Mr) of X? (Ar: C=12, H=1, O=16)
NSAA-2022-CHEM-010: Element Z is in Group 1 of the Periodic Table. A pure sample of Z consists of two isotopes with mass numbers 85 and 87, and has a relative atomic mass of 85.5. Which of the following statements is/are correct about element Z in this sample? (1) Z reacts with bromine to form an ionic compound with formula ZBr2; (2) Z forms a basic oxide; (3) more than 70% of the atoms of Z have mass number 85.
NSAA-2022-CHEM-011: Three mixtures (P, Q and R) of amino acids were separated using paper chromatography [DIAGRAM: chromatogram with component spots and Rf reference data from a first test]. The test was repeated with the same mixtures, paper and solvent; the common component travelled 7.5 cm. How far did the most mobile component of mixture Q travel in the second test?
NSAA-2022-CHEM-012: A sample of dry air at room temperature and pressure contains 25.0 mol of gas in total. Gas X contributes 1.50×10²³ particles. Gas Y would, alone, occupy 468 dm³ at rtp. What are the identities of X and Y, and the total amount of the remaining gases? (Avogadro's number = 6.00×10²³; 1 mol gas = 24.0 dm³ at rtp)
NSAA-2022-CHEM-013: The atomic number of fluorine is 9. Element X forms a fluoride XF3. Each molecule of XF3 has 32 electrons in total. Element X has two isotopes: one with equal protons and neutrons, the other with one more neutron than protons. The relative abundance of the heavier isotope is 0.80 (80%). What is the relative atomic mass of element X?
NSAA-2022-CHEM-014: 1 mol of compound X undergoes complete combustion to produce 144 dm³ of CO2 (at rtp). 1 mol of X can also undergo an addition reaction with 1 mol of hydrogen to form a saturated compound with one branch. X undergoes addition polymerisation; a section of the polymer containing three repeating units has an Mr between 200 and 300. [DIAGRAM: five candidate structural formulae A-E]. Which structural formula could be compound X? (Ar: C=12, H=1, F=19; 1 mol gas=24 dm³ at rtp)
NSAA-2022-CHEM-015: The equation shows complete combustion of an alkane: alkane + aO2 → bCO2 + cH2O. 100 cm³ of a gaseous alkane requires 650 cm³ of oxygen for complete combustion (same T, P). What is the value of a + b + c?
NSAA-2022-CHEM-016: A sample of magnesium carbonate, MgCO3, was reacted completely with 50 cm³ of 0.10 mol dm⁻³ hydrochloric acid, which is an excess. The remaining hydrochloric acid was titrated with 0.20 mol dm⁻³ sodium hydroxide solution; 5.0 cm³ of NaOH was required for complete neutralisation. What was the original mass of magnesium carbonate used, in mg? (Mr: MgCO3=84)
NSAA-2022-CHEM-017: A student mixed 30.0 cm³ of 3.0 mol dm⁻³ hydrochloric acid and 20.0 cm³ of 4.0 mol dm⁻³ aqueous ammonia in an insulated container. Initial temperature of both solutions was 20.0°C; the maximum temperature observed was 40.0°C. Specific heat capacity of solution = 4.0 J g⁻¹°C⁻¹; density = 1.0 g cm⁻³. What is the molar enthalpy change, in kJ mol⁻¹, for the reaction?
NSAA-2022-CHEM-018: An oxide of nitrogen can be prepared by the reaction of copper with hot nitric acid. The other products are copper(II) nitrate and water. 0.060 mol of copper reacted exactly with 40.0 cm³ of 4.00 mol dm⁻³ nitric acid. What is the empirical formula of the oxide of nitrogen produced?
NSAA-2022-CHEM-019: One mole of an unsaturated hydrocarbon reacts with exactly one mole of bromine to form a compound that contains carbon, hydrogen and bromine in the mass ratio 6:1:8 (by 1/15 fractions). What is the relative molar mass (Mr) of this product? (Ar: C=12, H=1, Br=80)
NSAA-2022-CHEM-020: Airbags contain sodium azide (NaN3) and potassium nitrate (KNO3). NaN3 decomposes: 2NaN3 → 2Na + 3N2. The sodium then reacts with excess KNO3: 10Na + 2KNO3 → K2O + 5Na2O + N2. An airbag contains 130 g of sodium azide. What is the total volume of nitrogen gas formed, at rtp? (Ar: N=14.0, Na=23.0; 1 mol gas=24.0 dm³ at rtp)
NSAA-2022-BIO-001: Which of the following is/are correct when a healthy human breathes in? (1) The ribcage moves up and out because air enters the lungs. (2) The volume of the thorax decreases and the thoracic pressure increases. (3) Energy is required to contract the intercostal muscles but not the diaphragm.
NSAA-2022-BIO-002: Which of the following statements about the cardiovascular system in a healthy individual is/are correct? (1) When blood flow in the capillaries is restricted, the rate of oxygen exchanged with the tissues is reduced. (2) Oxygen exchange from the blood in the arteries into the tissues is fast due to the high pressure of blood. (3) Capillary walls contain a small amount of smooth muscle to constrict the vessels.
NSAA-2022-BIO-003: An arrow shows the net movement of molecule X out of a healthy mammalian cell, from 0.05 mol dm⁻³ (outside) region indicated moving to where concentration outside is 0.10 mol dm⁻³ [DIAGRAM: cell with concentration gradient]. The maximum cell width is 10 μm. The cell membrane accounts for 0.2% of this width. What is the width of a single cell membrane, in nm, and which process is represented by the arrow?
NSAA-2022-BIO-004: Which one of the following comparisons is correct? A: alveoli/bronchi both tissues specialised for gas exchange. B: pancreas/ovary both organs that function as endocrine glands. C: phloem/xylem both organs that transport liquids from leaves to roots in plants. D: sensory neurone/motor neurone both tissues stimulated by a relay neurone. E: small intestine/trachea both organs that have tissues with cilia.
NSAA-2022-BIO-005: A graph shows cumulative frequency for heights of 15-year-old male and female students [DIAGRAM: two cumulative frequency curves]. Which of the following statements is/are correct? (1) The difference between mean heights of male and female students can be found by reading the difference in heights at a cumulative frequency of 12.5. (2) The difference in height between different males is explained by environmental factors alone. (3) The difference in the cumulative frequency graphs for males and females could be explained by a gene on the Y chromosome.
NSAA-2022-BIO-006: A student's drawing shows part of a reflex arc for pulling a hand away from a hot object, with labels: hormone; motor neurone; chemical receptors present on the membrane; sensory neurone; 'electrical impulse jumps across the gap between the two neurones' [DIAGRAM: reflex arc with labels A-E at different points]. Which label (A-E) is correct?
NSAA-2022-BIO-007: A cell from a healthy animal was removed. This cell contained four times the mass of DNA compared to a single gamete from the same animal. Which of the following statements could be correct? (1) The cell was just about to start meiosis. (2) The cell was just about to start mitosis. (3) The cell had just started meiosis. (4) The cell had just started mitosis.
NSAA-2022-BIO-008: Many species of cichlid fish have evolved from a common ancestor in Lake Victoria, each feeding on different food sources [DIAGRAM: four cichlid species with different feeding adaptations]. Which of the following statements about evolution of the cichlid fish is/are correct? (1) Different food types being available caused mutations in the DNA of the ancestral population of fish. (2) Natural selection was possible because there were multiple alleles of the genes affecting the traits shown in the diagram. (3) Natural selection of individuals with phenotypes best suited to different parts of the environment took place.
NSAA-2022-BIO-009: A graph shows the number of live bacteria growing in a nutrient broth at 30°C over 60 hours [DIAGRAM: growth curve rising then plateauing/declining]. Which statement is correct?
NSAA-2022-BIO-010:
Some students collected data using a belt transect on a beach and recorded the results in a kite diagram. The shading represents the number of individuals of each species present at each point along the transect (the wider the shading vertically, the larger the number of individuals; the vertical scale is the same for all species). The six species shown (Q, R, S, T, V, W) were the only ones present, and data was recorded every 5 m from 0 m to 50 m.
[DIAGRAM: a kite diagram with six horizontal rows (species Q, R, S, T, V, W) plotted against distance (0-50 m). Species Q is present (with varying abundance, peaking near 10 m) from 0 m up to about 28-30 m, then dies out. Species R starts at 10 m, peaks in abundance around 15-20 m, and tapers away to zero by about 40 m. Species S starts at 20 m, generally increases in abundance, and is still present at 50 m. Species T, V and W all begin at 30 m and continue to 50 m, but with visibly different abundance patterns: T's shading widens steadily from 30 m to a maximum at 50 m; V's and W's shading each widen to a peak (around 35-40 m) and then narrow again, but their peak widths and shapes differ from each other and from T's.]
Which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. The sampling site with the highest number of different species present is at 35 m.
2. The abundance of species T, V and W is the same.
3. At a distance of 5 m, species Q is the only species present.
NSAA-2022-BIO-011: The effect of pH on the breakdown of starch by pancreatic amylase was investigated. Starch and amylase were mixed at different pH values; percentage of starch remaining after 5 minutes was recorded [DIAGRAM: graph showing minimum starch remaining around pH 7, more remaining at pH 4.5 and pH 9]. Which of the following conclusions could be correct? (1) During the 5 minutes, there are more enzyme-product complexes formed at pH 4.5 than at pH 6. (2) The shape of the enzyme is different at pH 5 compared to pH 7. (3) The optimum pH for this enzyme is approximately pH 7 under the conditions of this experiment.
NSAA-2022-BIO-012: A diagram summarises homeostatic control of blood glucose concentration: fall in blood glucose → endocrine gland V → hormone W produced → target organ liver → release of glucose → process Y → normal blood glucose. Rise in blood glucose → hormone X produced → target organ liver → uptake of glucose → process Z → normal blood glucose. Which row is correct for gland V, hormone W, hormone X, process Y, process Z?
NSAA-2022-BIO-013: A graph shows the rate of transpiration at different temperatures for a plant in its natural environment [DIAGRAM: rate of transpiration rising from 20°C to 30°C]. Which of the following statements is/are correct? (1) Stomata open when the guard cells lose water and become flaccid. (2) Between 20°C and 27°C, the humidity around the leaves must have decreased. (3) The air speed around the leaves could be higher at 23°C than at 30°C.
NSAA-2022-BIO-014: An investigation studied the effect of protease concentration on the rate of protein breakdown. Results: concentration 10,20,30,40,50,60,70 (%) gave rate 1.8, 3.5, 5.3, 6.0, 6.3, 6.3, 6.3 (arbitrary units). Which of the following statements is/are correct? (1) Between 10% and 40%, the rate has increased as more active sites are available. (2) Between 50% and 70%, the enzyme concentration is the limiting factor. (3) At 60% protease concentration, the rate will be faster if substrate concentration is increased.
NSAA-2022-BIO-015: Samples of the same pond plant were kept underwater and exposed to five different colours of light at the same intensity. The time taken to release 20 bubbles was recorded [DIAGRAM: bar chart, shortest time for red and blue light, longest for green light]. Which of the following conclusions is/are correct? (1) This pond plant photosynthesises fastest when exposed to green light. (2) When exposed to any of these colours of light, this pond plant would release only oxygen. (3) When exposed to blue light, this pond plant produces 30 bubbles per minute.
NSAA-2022-BIO-016: Two sets of apparatus, P and Q, at the start: P has distilled water inside a partially permeable membrane bag, surrounded by distilled water; Q has distilled water, starch and amylase inside the membrane bag, surrounded by distilled water and starch [DIAGRAM: two osmometer setups]. The membrane is impermeable to starch/amylase but permeable to smaller molecules. After 10 minutes, meniscus in P moved up, meniscus in Q moved down. Which statement(s) correctly explain these observations? (1) Water moved by osmosis in P and Q. (2) Amylase was a substrate for starch. (3) Maltose was diffusing across the partially permeable membrane in Q, but not in P.
NSAA-2022-BIO-017: Two flies, both heterozygous for a trait controlled by a single gene (one dominant, one recessive allele), mated and all eggs were collected; offspring grew into young adult flies. Which of the following statements, taken independently, could be correct for the young adult fly population? (1) If homozygous dominant genotype stops eggs hatching, the genotypic ratio in young adults would be 1:1. (2) If homozygous recessive genotype produces sterile individuals, all young adult flies will have the same phenotype. (3) If the population of young adult flies is small, the phenotypic ratio would be 1:2:1.
NSAA-2022-BIO-018: A diagram shows part of sperm production: spermatogonium (stem cell) → primary spermatocyte → spermatids → sperm cells, with one pair of homologous chromosomes shown [DIAGRAM: spermatogenesis stages]. Which of the following statements is/are correct? (1) Gametes are produced from haploid stem cells. (2) Upon fertilisation, it is the male gamete that would determine the sex of the offspring. (3) DNA in the primary spermatocyte is copied before the spermatids are made.
NSAA-2022-BIO-019: A graph shows the effect of increasing light intensity on the net rate at which a plant releases oxygen into the atmosphere, with a point X where the net rate is zero [DIAGRAM: net O2 release vs light intensity, crossing zero at point X]. Which of the following could explain why the net rate of oxygen release at point X is zero? (1) The light intensity is too low to produce oxygen through photosynthesis. (2) All of the stomata will be closed at this light intensity, preventing the release of oxygen. (3) The rate of oxygen release from photosynthesis is equal to the rate of oxygen use in respiration.
NSAA-2022-BIO-020: Two parents are heterozygous for a recessive condition controlled by a single autosomal gene (one dominant, one recessive allele). They have a daughter who does not display the condition. This daughter has a child with a heterozygous man. What is the probability that this child displays the condition? (Assume no mutations.)
